name: react-dropzone (File Drop Zone for React)

auth.notes: UI component only; upload to any backend

agent_readiness.notes: Simple, minimal React file drop zone. useDropzone() hook: getRootProps(), getInputProps(), isDragActive, acceptedFiles. accept: {'image/*': ['.jpg', '.png']}. maxFiles, maxSize, minSize, multiple. onDrop callback with accepted/rejected files. Headless (no CSS). You handle the upload — react-dropzone handles only the file selection/drop UI. Very small (~2KB). Used by thousands of React apps. Pair with your HTTP client or cloud SDK for actual upload.

gotchas: Headless — you must provide all styling
No built-in upload functionality (just file selection)
File validation is client-side only (validate server-side too)
best_when: React apps needing simple drag-and-drop file selection
Custom-styled upload UIs where Uppy's Dashboard is too opinionated
File inputs needing type/size validation before upload
Lightweight alternative to Uppy for simple drop zones
avoid_when: Need built-in upload UI with progress (use Uppy)
Multiple source integration (Drive, Dropbox) — use Uppy
summary: react-dropzone is a minimal headless React hook for drag-and-drop file selection. useDropzone() returns getRootProps/getInputProps, isDragActive, acceptedFiles. Accept types, max size, multiple files. ~2KB. No upload logic — just file selection UI. Pair with axios/fetch/SDK for actual uploads. Best for custom-styled file inputs in React apps. Use Uppy when you need a full-featured upload widget.
